description: Zimbabwe 10 ZiG banknote issued in 2024 as a gold-backed currency featuring Chiremba balancing rocks and QR code authentication.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe introduced the 10 ZiG note on April 5, 2024, as part of the initial launch of the gold-backed ZiG currency system. This dark blue and red banknote measures 155 x 65 mm and bears the signature of Dr. John Mushayavanhu. The obverse displays the iconic Chiremba balancing rocks located in Epworth, representing Zimbabwe's natural heritage and geological significance. A QR code on the obverse provides instant verification of authenticity when scanned, returning a plaintext confirmation string identifying the denomination and issuing authority.

The reverse side depicts molten gold pouring into an ingot mold atop stacked gold bars, symbolizing Zimbabwe's mineral wealth and the currency's gold-backing mechanism. Both sides feature the Zimbabwe Bird as a registration device. Security features include a green-to-blue windowed security thread with demetalized RBZZIG inscription and a Zimbabwe Bird watermark. The 10 ZiG was one of only two denominations that actually entered circulation; higher denominations were postponed to prevent inflation concerns.

highlights.design: Front: Chiremba balancing rocks, Zimbabwe Bird registration device, QR code. Back: Molten gold into ingot mold, gold bar stack, Zimbabwe Bird registration device.
highlights.reason: Initial denomination of the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's new gold-backed currency system launched April 5, 2024 to replace hyperinflated Zimbabwean dollar.
highlights.security: Green-to-blue windowed security thread with RBZZIG demetalization, Zimbabwe Bird watermark, QR code with plaintext verification string, registration devices with Zimbabwe Bird.

description: Zimbabwe 20 ZiG banknote issued in 2024 featuring Great Zimbabwe ruins and Victoria Falls.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe released the 20 ZiG note on April 5, 2024, as the second active denomination of the newly launched gold-backed currency. This banknote measures approximately 145 mm by 70 mm and incorporates designs celebrating Zimbabwe's most significant cultural and natural landmarks. The obverse prominently displays the Great Zimbabwe ruins, an UNESCO World Heritage Site representing the nation's pre-colonial architectural achievement and served as the capital of the Medieval Kingdom of Zimbabwe. The reverse showcases the majestic Victoria Falls, one of the world's most spectacular natural wonders located on the Zambezi River between Zimbabwe and Zambia.

The banknote's enhanced security features include a dynamic holographic security strip running alongside the central motif, providing sophisticated anti-counterfeiting protection. The serial number appears in striking red font, contributing both to visual appeal and security. The design is further secured by a detailed Zimbabwe Bird watermark that is aligned precisely across both obverse and reverse surfaces. The 20 ZiG represents the second denomination to achieve successful circulation, alongside the 10 ZiG note.

highlights.design: Front: Great Zimbabwe archaeological ruins. Back: Victoria Falls landscape with Zambezi River.
highlights.reason: Second denomination of the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's gold-backed ZiG currency system launched April 5, 2024.
highlights.security: Dynamic holographic security strip, red serial number with anti-counterfeiting properties, Zimbabwe Bird watermark with precise front-to-back alignment.

description: Zimbabwe 50 ZiG banknote planned for 2024 but not issued; released in 2026 as part of the upgraded BiG5 series.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe announced the 50 ZiG denomination as part of the original eight-denomination ZiG banknote series unveiled on April 5, 2024. However, the Reserve Bank postponed its release on May 30, 2024, citing inflationary concerns that higher denomination circulation could destabilize the economy. The 50 ZiG was held in reserve during the initial 2024 phase, along with the 100 and 200 ZiG denominations, while only the 10 and 20 ZiG entered circulation.

By early 2026, economic conditions had improved significantly, with official inflation falling to 3.8 percent, enabling the Reserve Bank to reconsider its cautious approach. The 50 ZiG was finally released on April 7, 2026, as part of the new "BiG5" series (second generation), featuring enhanced security features and updated designs compared to the original 2024 specifications. This delayed emission reflected monetary policy priorities rather than design or production issues.

highlights.design: Part of ZiG series design family with enhanced 2026 specifications.
highlights.reason: Postponed from 2024 due to inflation concerns; eventually issued April 7, 2026 as part of upgraded BiG5 series once economic conditions stabilized.
highlights.security: Enhanced security features as part of 2026 BiG5 series standards.

description: Zimbabwe 100 ZiG banknote planned for 2024 but not issued; scheduled for later release in 2026 as part of the BiG5 series.
body:
The 100 ZiG denomination was included in the original comprehensive ZiG banknote series unveiled by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe on April 5, 2024. Like the 50 and 200 ZiG notes, its release was postponed indefinitely on May 30, 2024, as the monetary authorities determined that introducing higher denominations could exacerbate inflationary pressures during the critical early phase of the new currency's adoption. The Reserve Bank's cautious approach prioritized currency stabilization by limiting circulation to smaller denominations (10 and 20 ZiG).

As of early 2026, the 100 ZiG remained in planned-but-not-yet-released status, with the Reserve Bank indicating its eventual inclusion in subsequent phases of the BiG5 series rollout. The specific timeline for 100 ZiG circulation has not been finalized, representing continued caution regarding higher-value note circulation despite improved macroeconomic indicators.

highlights.design: High-denomination note within ZiG series portfolio.
highlights.reason: Postponed from 2024 launch due to inflation control measures; future release date to be determined by Reserve Bank monetary policy.
highlights.security: To be determined upon eventual 2026+ release.

description: Zimbabwe 200 ZiG banknote planned for 2024 but not issued; future release contingent on monetary stability.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe included the 200 ZiG as the highest denomination in the original eight-denomination ZiG banknote series announced on April 5, 2024. Its release was postponed on May 30, 2024, alongside the 50 and 100 ZiG notes, as the Reserve Bank determined that introducing large-value denominations during the currency's initial phase could fuel inflationary expectations and destabilize the ZiG's market acceptance. The Reserve Bank's decision reflected monetary policy conservatism aimed at ensuring the gold-backed currency achieved credibility before higher denominations entered circulation.

As of 2026, the 200 ZiG has not been released and remains in planned status. The Reserve Bank has indicated that higher denominations may eventually be introduced in the BiG5 or subsequent series, but only when macroeconomic conditions and currency stability metrics fully support such action. This represents the most conservative approach to banknote denomination strategy in the ZiG currency's rollout.

highlights.design: Highest-denomination note within original ZiG series design family.
highlights.reason: Postponed from 2024 launch to prevent inflation; conditional future release pending monetary stability verification.
highlights.security: To be determined upon potential future release.

issuer: National Bank of the Republic of Abkhazia
description: Abkhazia 25 Apsar commemorative banknote issued May 22, 2023, celebrating the 30th anniversary of the Patriotic War victory.
body:
The National Bank of the Republic of Abkhazia issued the 25 Apsar commemorative banknote on May 22, 2023, in commemoration of the 30th anniversary of the Patriotic War of the People of Abkhazia (1992–1993). This historic warfare resulted in de facto Abkhazian independence and remains central to national identity. The banknote measures 150 mm x 65 mm and was executed on white cotton paper with a limited mintage of 15,000 pieces not distributed for general circulation, maintaining its collector status.

The obverse side displays the Abkhazian coat of arms, a traditional sailing ship representing maritime heritage, ornamental border designs, a hawk in flight symbolizing national pride and freedom, stars representing the night sky, and a detailed hawk head in profile. The reverse depicts two standing soldiers in military uniform holding swords and a rifle, commemorating the combatants of the 1992-1993 conflict, alongside matching stars and traditional ornamental patterns. A QR code in the lower right of the reverse provides a link to the National Bank website with detailed security feature descriptions. The banknote employs distinctive orange, yellow, and green coloration.

highlights.design: Obverse: Coat of arms, sailing ship, hawk in flight, stars, hawk profile. Reverse: Two soldiers with weapons, stars, ornamental patterns, QR code.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issuance for 30th anniversary of 1992–1993 Patriotic War victory; limited edition (15,000 pieces) not for general circulation.
highlights.security: Watermark with basket weave pattern, two types of protective fibers, color-shifting HMC (hidden multi-colour) security element, Orlov effect in offset and metallographic printing, graphic guilloche elements, QR code for verification.

description: Angola 1,000 Kwanzas polymer banknote issued November 2023 featuring Kalandula waterfalls.
body:
The Banco Nacional de Angola issued the 1,000 Kwanzas banknote in November 2023 as part of the ongoing modernization of Angola's currency with polymer substrate materials. The note is colored pink and features Kalandula waterfalls, one of Africa's highest waterfalls located in Malanje Province, representing Angola's natural landmarks and tourism assets. The polymer construction ensures superior durability compared to traditional paper notes, extending the banknote's circulation lifespan to approximately four times longer than conventional fiber-based currency.

The banknote incorporates two transparent windows cut into the polymer substrate: the first contains a semitransparent representation of "The Thinker" (O Pensador), a national cultural symbol representing Angolan intellectual heritage, with the denomination printed in translucent ink. The second window features a geometric figure representing the traditional musical instrument "Omakola," reflecting Angolan cultural heritage and musical traditions. The polymer substrate and clear windows represent contemporary anti-counterfeiting and security advances adopted throughout Angola's modern banknote series, with denominations from 200 to 2,000 kwanzas utilizing polymer technology.

highlights.design: Pink-colored polymer note. Front: Kalandula waterfalls, transparent window with Thinker figure. Back: Traditional Omakola instrument in geometric window.
highlights.reason: Part of Banco Nacional de Angola's ongoing currency modernization program beginning in 2020, upgrading to durable polymer substrate with enhanced security features.
highlights.security: Two transparent polymer windows (Thinker figure, Omakola musical instrument representation), polymer substrate for enhanced durability, translucent ink printing.

description: Angola 2,000 Kwanzas polymer banknote featuring Serra da Leba landscape and Thinker cultural symbol.
body:
The Banco Nacional de Angola issued the 2,000 Kwanzas banknote on November 11, 2020, as part of the comprehensive redesign of Angola's banknote series using polymer substrates. The note is colored in shades of green, with imagery paying homage to Serra da Leba, a dramatic mountain pass and natural wonder in Huíla Province featuring distinctive geological formations and scenic vistas. The polymer material provides enhanced durability, environmental resistance, and counterfeit prevention compared to traditional paper notes.

The banknote design incorporates two distinct transparent windows cut into the polymer substrate. The primary window features a semitransparent representation of "The Thinker" (O Pensador), a significant cultural emblem within Angolan artistic and intellectual traditions, with the banknote denomination printed in translucent ink for visual effect. A second geometric window represents the shape of the "Omakola," a traditional Angolan musical instrument, celebrating the nation's rich musical heritage and cultural identity. The inclusion of these cultural symbols on polymer currency reflects the Banco Nacional de Angola's commitment to preserving national heritage while advancing security technology.

highlights.design: Green-colored polymer note. Front: Serra da Leba mountain landscape. Back: Transparent windows featuring Thinker figure and Omakola musical instrument.
highlights.reason: Issued November 11, 2020, as part of Banco Nacional de Angola's comprehensive polymer banknote series redesign; symbolizes national pride and technological advancement.
highlights.security: Transparent polymer windows with cultural imagery (Thinker figure, Omakola instrument), polymer substrate material for durability and counterfeit resistance, translucent ink effects.

issuer: Banco Central de la República Argentina
description: Argentina 1,000 Pesos banknote issued 2023, part of ongoing currency update series featuring native Hornero bird.
body:
The Banco Central de la República Argentina released a new 1,000 Pesos banknote variant in 2023 as part of ongoing currency design updates and security enhancements. The banknote continues the established Fourth Series design family, prominently featuring the Rufous Hornero (Furnarius rufus), Argentina's national bird, in its natural habitat within the Pampas grasslands. The obverse side displays a detailed portrait of the hornero alongside native plants, bird paw prints, bird's nests, branches, and flowers that create a naturalistic representation of Pampean ecology. The reverse side showcases the geographic landscape of the Pampas region with a cartographic representation of Argentina's national territory.

This 1,000 Pesos note represents the highest-value denomination in Argentina's active circulation and reflects the central bank's commitment to featuring indigenous wildlife as national symbols. The banknote incorporates multiple security features including a hologram security thread, advanced printing techniques, and tamper-evident design elements. The red-orange-yellow color scheme creates distinctive visual identification while maintaining compatibility with the broader Fourth Series design language established when the denomination was first introduced in 2017.

highlights.design: Red-orange-yellow colored note. Obverse: Rufous Hornero national bird, nest, paw prints, flora. Reverse: Pampas landscape and map of Argentina.
highlights.reason: Continuing circulation of Argentina's highest denomination; 2023 variant updates security features and design elements while maintaining Fourth Series family consistency.
highlights.security: Hologram security thread, advanced printing techniques, tamper-evident design elements, precise registration features.

issuer: Banco Central de la República Argentina
description: Argentina 2,000 Pesos commemorative banknote issued May 22, 2023, celebrating development of science and medicine in Argentina.
body:
The Banco Central de la República Argentina issued the 2,000 Pesos banknote on May 22, 2023, as a commemorative note honoring the Development of Science and Medicine in Argentina. This represents the highest denomination banknote ever circulated in Argentine currency history. The note measures 155 mm x 65 mm and features a dark gray and pink color scheme with signatures of C. Moreau and Pesce.

The obverse prominently displays portraits of two pioneering figures in Argentine medicine: Dr. Cecilia Grierson, the first woman to graduate as a physician in Argentina (1889) and founder of Latin America's first formally structured nursing school, and Dr. Ramón Carrillo, Argentina's first Minister of Health (1946–1954). Additional obverse imagery includes molecular representations, a caduceus symbol in green-to-blue optically variable ink, and modern hospital buildings symbolizing contemporary medical infrastructure. The reverse depicts the National Institute of Microbiology in Buenos Aires, a leading research institution. Dr. Grierson's pioneering work in obstetrics and nursing education established foundational standards for healthcare professionalism in Latin America, while Dr. Carrillo's tenure as Health Minister eliminated malaria, eradicated endemic diseases, and dramatically reduced infant mortality through public health innovation.

highlights.design: Dark gray and pink note. Obverse: Dr. Cecilia Grierson and Dr. Ramón Carrillo portraits, molecule, caduceus, hospitals. Reverse: National Institute of Microbiology building.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issuance for development of science and medicine in Argentina; highest-denomination banknote in Argentine circulation; issued May 22, 2023.
highlights.security: Watermark of Dr. Grierson and Dr. Carrillo, green-to-blue caduceus OVI, identification code with tactile relief, microprinting, latent images.
